questions.q: 'Hoeveel kinderen heb jij?' — why is it 'heb' and not 'hebt'?
options: Inversion: 'jij' after the verb drops the -t
questions.explanation: When 'jij/je' comes AFTER the verb (inversion in questions), the -t drops. 'heb jij?' not 'hebt jij?'.
'geen' negates nouns. 'Ik heb geen [noun]' = I have no [noun].

questions.q: How do you say 'My oldest is twelve years old'?
questions.explanation: 'oudste' = oldest. Dutch uses 'zijn' for age: 'is twaalf jaar', not 'heeft twaalf jaar'.

questions.q: You're in a shop. How do you ask 'How much does it cost?'
questions.explanation: 'Hoeveel kost het?' and 'Wat kost het?' are both natural and common.

questions.q: The price is too high. What do you say?
questions.explanation: 'te duur' = too expensive. 'goedkoop' = cheap. 'een koopje' = a bargain.

questions.q: 'Hoeveel kosten die?' — when do you use 'kosten' instead of 'kost'?
questions.explanation: 'kost' = singular. 'kosten' = plural. Match the verb to the subject, not the price.

options: 'één' = the number one (with accent), 'een' = the article a/an
questions.explanation: The accent marks on 'één' distinguish the number from the article 'een' (a/an).

questions.q: 'Ik heb één broer en twee zussen' — what does this mean?
options: I have two brothers and one sister
I have one brother and two sisters
